mechanism_narrative: A lower 3-month change in FEDFUNDS signals easier discount-rate conditions and abundant liquidity, raising equity valuation multiples and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing or zero-rate policy regime
failure_condition: Reject if lower 3-month FEDFUNDS changes are followed by non-positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a high threshold signals deteriorating household income and recession risk, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 8.0% and rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS rises while UNRATE falls, improving consumer demand and labor income reinforce each other, increasing expected corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth crosses above 0% while UNRATE 3-month change crosses below 0pp
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value is 406748.0 and UNRATE is 8.1
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-up and UNRATE-down condition does not outperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return by at least 1 percentage point on average.

mechanism_narrative: A negative turn in INDPRO growth signals weakening production, capacity utilization, and cyclical earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside crossings are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST from depressed levels signals improving construction demand and durable-goods spillovers, raising cyclical earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below 800 and its 3-month growth crosses above 10%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST rebounds from below 800 fail to deliver a positive average 60-day S&P 500 return in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS rises, tighter monetary reaction to inflation increases discount rates and lowers equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS 3-month change crosses above 0.25pp while CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds its prior 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value is 230.102 and FEDFUNDS is 0.13
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI acceleration and FEDFUNDS tightening condition produces non-negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns or a hit rate below 50% for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Positive PAYEMS momentum signals expanding payroll income and improving aggregate demand, increasing expected revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month PAYEMS growth has a negative walk-forward Rank IC with 5-day S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth are both positive, broad real activity and current retail demand confirm an earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth crosses above 0% while RSAFS 3-month growth is above 0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value is 13564.5 and RSAFS is 406748.0
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive GDPC1 and RSAFS growth does not produce higher average 60-day S&P 500 returns than periods when either series has non-positive growth.
